prompt: Pin down the EXACT EU AI Act and GPAI obligation dates and WHICH BUYER ROLE owns the compliance budget, from primary EU sources. From artificialintelligenceact.eu, the official EUR-Lex text, and the EU AI Office, list verbatim with article numbers and dates: (a) the GPAI provider obligations effective date(s) and any Code of Practice deadlines; (b) the high-risk system obligations effective date (Aug 2 2026 vs Aug 2 2027 distinction by Annex), including Article 12 logging; (c) penalty amounts and effective dates. Then, separately, determine from 2026 buyer-side sources WHICH internal role/budget line funds AI Act readiness spend (Chief Compliance Officer, CISO, DPO, General Counsel, Head of AI Governance) and the typical budget size, with cited evidence (@handle+date or URL). Conclude: which single date is the sharpest budget-unlock for a kolm-style readiness product and which job title to address urgency copy to. Cite every date; flag any uncertainty about scope or whether a date has shifted.
content:
**Primary sources confirm the following verbatim or near-verbatim details (primarily from artificialintelligenceact.eu summaries of the Act text, cross-referenced with EUR-Lex Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 and related EU pages; no material shifts reported as of June 2026).**[[1]](https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/implementation-timeline/)[[2]](https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/the-act/)[[1]](https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/implementation-timeline/)

**(a) GPAI provider obligations (Chapter V, Articles 51–56, including obligations in Articles 53 and 55 for systemic-risk models):**  
Application date: 2 August 2025 (new models placed on the market/put into service from this date).  
Pre-2 August 2025 models: Providers must take necessary steps to comply by 2 August 2027.  
Codes of Practice (Article 56): Commission deadline for readiness 2 May 2025 (voluntary tool; published July 2025 and confirmed adequate by Commission/AI Board). If inadequate, Commission may adopt implementing acts by 2 August 2025. Full enforcement powers (e.g., fines, information requests) align with penalties from 2 August 2025/2026.[[1]](https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/implementation-timeline/)[[3]](https://www.kennedyslaw.com/en/thought-leadership/article/2026/the-eu-ai-act-implementation-timeline-understanding-the-next-deadline-for-compliance/)

**(b) High-risk AI system obligations (Chapter III, e.g., Articles 8–27 for providers/deployers, including risk management, data governance, technical documentation, transparency, human oversight, etc.):**  
General application: 2 August 2026 (remainder of the Act, except specific provisions).  
Distinction by Annex/Article 6: Article 6(1) (Annex III high-risk systems) and corresponding obligations apply from 2 August 2027. High-risk systems already on the market before 2 August 2026 are subject only if subject to significant design changes thereafter.  
Article 12 (Record-Keeping/logging): Applies from 2 August 2026 (“High-risk AI systems shall technically allow for the automatic recording of events (logs) over the lifetime of the system...” with specific requirements for traceability). Deployers retain logs for at least six months in relevant cases.[[1]](https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/implementation-timeline/)[[4]](https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/article/12/)[[5]](https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/article/6/)

**(c) Penalties (Articles 99–101, Chapter XII enforcement):**  
Effective date: 2 August 2025 (Articles 99 and 100 apply from this date; Member States must lay down rules by then).  
Amounts (Article 99):  
- Non-compliance with prohibited practices (Article 5): up to €35,000,000 or 7% of total worldwide annual turnover (whichever higher).  
- Breaches of high-risk obligations or other requirements: up to €15,000,000 or 3% of turnover.  
- Providing incorrect/misleading information to authorities: up to €7,500,000 or 1% of turnover.  
SMEs receive lower fines; severity factors apply. Member States report annually. GPAI-specific fines (Article 101) up to 3% or €15M.[[6]](https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/article/99/)[[1]](https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/implementation-timeline/)

**Uncertainties flagged:** Exact scope for certain Annex III sub-items or legacy systems may involve national guidance or Commission guidelines (e.g., on Article 6); no date shifts confirmed. GPAI enforcement ramps up fully in 2026 alongside high-risk rules. Article 113 of the Act sets the phased application dates referenced above.

**Buyer-side sources (2026 reports/summaries on readiness spend):**  
Ownership is not uniformly assigned to one role and varies by organization size/sector, but evidence points most frequently to **Chief Compliance Officer / GRC (Governance, Risk, Compliance) leaders or dedicated AI governance/compliance functions** (often overlapping with or reporting to General Counsel or CISO). Examples include:  
- CISO/GRC leaders explicitly positioned for AI governance responsibilities under the Act (security + oversight of risk assessments, logging, etc.).[[7]](https://www.kovrr.com/blog-post/eu-ai-act-compliance-explained-for-cisos-and-grc-leaders)  
- Emphasis on named C-level accountability with budget authority (CIO/CDO/CAIO/CRO/compliance often cited; “Risk and Compliance Leadership typically chairs”).[[8]](https://trustible.ai/post/how-to-establish-an-effective-ai-governance-committee-in-2026/)[[9]](https://www.digital-chiefs.de/en/ai-governance-2026-only-14-percent-responsible/)  
- Dedicated AI governance budgets or GRC tech spend increases (72% of orgs expect GRC budget growth; AI governance as top priority).[[10]](https://optro.ai/blog/ai-governance-stats)  

Typical budget size: Significant but variable—large enterprises may face initial $8–15M investments for high-risk conformity (plus $1–5M annual); mid-size $2–5M initial. Broader AI governance platform spend projected at hundreds of millions market-wide in 2026, with dedicated lines recommended. No single universal figure; often under compliance/GRC or AI governance budgets rather than pure IT/security or legal alone.[[11]](https://labs.cloudsecurityalliance.org/research/csa-research-note-eu-ai-act-high-risk-compliance-deadline-20/)[[10]](https://optro.ai/blog/ai-governance-stats)

**Conclusion:** The sharpest budget-unlock date for a kolm-style readiness product is **2 August 2026** (high-risk obligations + ramped GPAI enforcement/penalties apply broadly, driving immediate spend on logging, documentation, assessments, etc., ahead of the 2027 Annex III tail). Address urgency copy to the **Chief Compliance Officer (or Head of AI Governance / GRC lead)**, as they most consistently own or heavily influence the relevant budget line per 2026 sources.
